content: Can I share something with you, gently? I might be wrong — and you can tell me if I am.
I want to be honest with you in a caring way, because I think there's more beneath the surface.
A lot of people say they're fine when they're not ready to say they're hurting. That doesn't make you weak — it makes you human.
You don't owe me an explanation. But if something is sitting heavy inside, I'm here — no judgment, no rush.
What would it feel like to tell me the truth about how today has really been?
If 'fine' isn't the whole story, what's the part you've been keeping to yourself?
